paper_evidence.summary: PKC412 effectively inhibited proliferation of OPM-1 multiple myeloma cells harboring FGFR3 K650E mutation, with dose-dependent reduction in cell viability and inhibition of FGFR3 autophosphorylation
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Cell viability reduced to ~25% at 1000nM PKC412

alignment.notes: Strong alignment between paper, GT and AI for core finding

paper_evidence.summary: N540D mutation confers resistance to PKC412 in Ba/F3 cells transformed with FGFR3 TDII K650E/N540D or TEL-FGFR3 N540D

alignment.notes: Clear resistance mechanism validated in paper

paper_evidence.summary: PKC412 effectively inhibited KMS11 multiple myeloma cells harboring FGFR3 Y373C mutation
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Cell viability reduced to ~40% at 400nM PKC412

alignment.notes: Clear sensitivity demonstrated in KMS11 cell line

ai_improvements_over_gt.paper_support: PKC412 showed significant survival benefit in murine model of FGFR3 TDII (K650E)-induced pre-B cell lymphoma (P=0.0006)
PKC412 showed efficacy against TEL-FGFR3 fusion in myeloproliferative disease model (P<0.0001)
Paper suggests PKC412 efficacy for t(4;12) TEL-FGFR3-induced peripheral T-cell lymphoma based on preclinical data

overall_judgement.summary: The AI extraction captured the key evidence items from the paper with high accuracy and identified several additional valid evidence items missing from ground truth. Both systems correctly identified the core findings around PKC412 sensitivity in FGFR3-mutant multiple myeloma and resistance mechanisms. The AI extraction provided more comprehensive coverage of disease contexts studied.
strengths: Accurate capture of core findings around drug sensitivity and resistance
Identification of additional valid evidence items missing from ground truth
Detailed extraction of quantitative data and experimental context
Clear delineation between different disease contexts
weaknesses: Some extracted evidence items combine findings from cell line and animal models without clear distinction
Confidence levels could be more precisely calibrated to strength of evidence
